habitat: Forest; woodland; plantations; gardens; coastal and riparian forests
story: An icon of the skies, the African Wood Owl (Strix woodfordii) is a species from the Strigidae family. It thrives in Forest; woodland; plantations; gardens; coastal and riparian forests, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the African Wood Owl is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the African Wood Owl helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the African Wood Owl, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
